title: Florence + The Machine: Everybody Scream Tour
description: Florence Welch brings her Everybody Scream era to Madison Square Garden for two consecutive nights. Raw, anthemic, and cathedral-loud — the kind of show that leaves you hoarse by the second song.
hook_text: Two nights at MSG and Florence Welch will make you feel every single one of them. This is the tour people will be talking about all summer.

title: SPRING/BREAK Art Show 2026
description: The curated alternative art fair returns to 625 Madison Avenue during Frieze Week. Known for its unconventional, installation-heavy approach, SPRING/BREAK consistently surfaces the most unexpected work in New York — artists curating entire rooms, not booths.
hook_text: Frieze gets the headlines but SPRING/BREAK is where the real edge lives — 100+ artist-curated rooms in a Midtown tower during art week. Half the price, twice the surprise.

title: Queens Night Market
description: The world's most culturally diverse night market returns to Flushing Meadows Corona Park for its 11th season. Every dish is capped at $6, and the 100+ vendors represent cuisines from more than 80 countries — Tibetan momos next to Peruvian anticuchos next to Nigerian jollof rice.
hook_text: Every dish $6 or under, every country on the menu. The Queens Night Market is the one food event in NYC that actually lives up to the hype — and it runs every Saturday night.

title: Becky Shaw on Broadway
description: Gina Gionfriddo's razor-sharp dark comedy gets its long-overdue Broadway debut at the Hayes Theatre. A dinner party spirals into something messier and far funnier — this is the kind of play that makes you rethink every person you've ever set a friend up with.
hook_text: A two-time Pulitzer finalist with the bite of a great roast — Becky Shaw is the sharpest comedy on Broadway right now and it closes June 14. Don't wait.

title: Maybe Happy Ending on Broadway
description: The six-Tony-winning musical tells the quietly devastating story of two decommissioned helper robots in a near-future Seoul apartment building. Poetic, inventive, and unlike anything else on Broadway — the kind of show people see twice.
hook_text: Six Tonys doesn't cover it — Maybe Happy Ending is the rare Broadway show that feels like a poem. Still running at the Belasco and worth every dollar to see it while you can.

title: Jerrod Carmichael at The Bell House
description: Emmy and Grammy Award-winner Jerrod Carmichael plays a rare intimate set at The Bell House in Brooklyn. After his HBO specials and high-profile hosting turns, catching him in a 500-seat room feels like an event in itself.
hook_text: One of the most important voices in comedy, in a 500-seat room in Gowanus. Jerrod Carmichael at The Bell House is the kind of ticket you'll wish you'd bought sooner.

title: Aimé Leon Dore Sample Sale
description: Aimé Leon Dore opens its annual sample sale at 260 Sample Sale with deep cuts on past-season knitwear, outerwear, and tailoring. Previous sales have moved signature pieces at 50–70% off retail — the cashmere, the archival denim, and the Clarks collaboration always go first.
hook_text: ALD sample sale — cashmere at 60% off doesn't happen twice. First-day access is worth every minute of the line.

title: Stüssy NYC Sample Sale
description: Stüssy opens a rare NYC sample sale at Soiffer Haskin with past-season pieces from their Spring/Summer and Fall/Winter runs — graphic tees, hoodies, outerwear, and accessories at deep discounts. The kind of sale that moves fast and leaves people wishing they'd shown up an hour earlier.
hook_text: Stüssy sample sale: archive graphics and outerwear at 60% off. These sell out before noon — get there early.

title: Tracksmith NYC Saturday Run Club
description: Tracksmith's East Village store hosts weekly Saturday morning runs through Tompkins Square Park and along the East River path. All paces welcome, free to join, coffee after at the store. The post-run community is half the point.
hook_text: Tracksmith's weekly runs leave the East Village store at 9am every Saturday — free, all paces, coffee after. Best thing you can do with a Saturday morning.

title: Barry's Outdoor Boot Camp
description: Barry's takes its signature boot camp outside for a limited spring pop-up series in Central Park. Same dual-format structure — intervals on turf, resistance work on the mat — but open air. RSVP through the Barry's app; class sizes are capped and these always sell out.
hook_text: Barry's brings the Red Room outside for a limited Central Park series. Same workout, open sky — RSVP through the app before they cap.
address: Sheep Meadow, Central Park, New York, NY 10024

title: Oriana Opens in Nolita
description: From the team behind Noortwyck, Oriana arrives at 174 Mott Street with a menu built around live-fire cooking — wood-grilled meats, open-flame vegetables, and a wine list that skews natural and low-intervention. Eater covered it before week one. Book early before the wait becomes brutal.
hook_text: Noortwyck's new Nolita restaurant is already on Eater's radar — wood-fire focused, natural wine, and exactly the kind of opening that books out within a week.

title: TV Girl at Elsewhere
description: LA bedroom-pop duo TV Girl headline the Hall at Elsewhere for a night of their signature lo-fi indie — dreamy samples, dry wit, and a catalog that sounds better at 11pm in Bushwick than anywhere else. Their NYC shows are always close to sold out.
hook_text: TV Girl at Elsewhere: the bedroom-pop duo whose catalog sounds better live than on record. Get tickets before the presale disappears.

title: John Summit at Avant Gardner
description: Chicago house DJ John Summit brings his relentless four-on-the-floor energy to the Great Hall at Avant Gardner. Deep tech-house with proper peak-time moments — the kind of set where you look up and it's already 4am and you don't care.
hook_text: John Summit at Avant Gardner: the most-streamed house DJ of the year in one of Brooklyn's best rooms. Pre-sale sells out in hours — don't wait on this one.

title: Dry Cleaning at Brooklyn Steel
description: London post-punk outfit Dry Cleaning bring their deadpan spoken-word style and motorik rhythms to Brooklyn Steel. Florence Shaw's detached delivery over angular guitars is one of the most singular live experiences in indie right now — the kind of show that stays with you for weeks.
hook_text: Dry Cleaning at Brooklyn Steel: post-punk deadpan from London that hits harder live than on record. One of the best shows you'll see in a room this size.

title: Metrograph: Agnès Varda Complete Retrospective
description: Metrograph runs a complete retrospective of Agnès Varda's 60-year career — from Cléo from 5 to 7 and Le Bonheur through The Gleaners and I and Faces Places. 35mm prints, DCP restorations, and two weeks of programming spanning her fiction and documentary work. One of the best curated cinema series you'll see in New York this year.
hook_text: Metrograph's full Varda retrospective: 35mm prints, six decades of work, two weeks only. The kind of movie-going that reminds you why cinema exists.

title: Artists & Fleas Chelsea
description: Chelsea's weekend design market reopens for spring with 75+ independent makers, vintage dealers, and small-batch food vendors in the space near the High Line. The quality gap between this and a regular flea is significant — it's where downtown designers sell excess, and where you find the piece you didn't know you needed.
hook_text: Artists & Fleas reopens for spring in Chelsea — 75 independent makers and vintage dealers, zero tourist energy. More like a pop-up concept store than a flea market.

title: Ruffian: Spring Wine List Launch
description: East Village wine bar Ruffian launches its spring list with a two-week pour schedule — Loire, Jura, Campania, and a handful of natural Burgundy producers they've been holding since last fall. Bar seats available walk-in; no reservation required. One of the best small wine bars in the city.
hook_text: Ruffian's spring list just dropped — Loire to Jura to Campania, all natural, walk-in bar seats available. Let them guide you through it.
